5 Signs You Need Professional Pest Control

Maria Santos — March 15, 2026

Recognizing when to call professional pest control services can save you time, money, and stress. While DIY solutions may work for minor pest problems, certain signs indicate that it's time to bring in the experts.

1. You've Tried DIY Methods Without Success

If you've attempted various over-the-counter solutions and the pest problem persists or worsens, it's time to call professionals. Expert pest control technicians have access to specialized products and techniques that are more effective than consumer-grade solutions.

2. You Notice Structural Damage

Termites, carpenter ants, and other wood-destroying pests can cause significant structural damage to your property. If you notice wood damage, hollow-sounding timber, or small holes in wood surfaces, immediate professional intervention is necessary to prevent further destruction.

3. You See Pest Droppings or Tracks

Finding pest droppings, tracks, or smear marks along walls and baseboards indicates an active infestation. These signs suggest that pests have established pathways in your home and are actively breeding, requiring comprehensive professional treatment.

4. You Experience Health Issues

Some pests can trigger allergies, asthma, or spread diseases. If family members experience unexplained health issues like skin irritation, respiratory problems, or allergic reactions, pest infestations could be the culprit. Professional pest control can identify and eliminate these health hazards.

5. You Spot Pests During Daytime

Many pests are nocturnal, so seeing them during daytime hours often indicates a severe infestation. When pest populations grow large enough to force some individuals out during the day, professional intervention is necessary to address the entire colony.

Don't wait until a minor pest problem becomes a major infestation. If you notice any of these warning signs, contact QUREIT Pest Control for a professional assessment. Our experienced technicians can quickly identify the extent of your pest problem and implement effective solutions to protect your home and family.
